title: acronychia goniocarpa
intro: Acronychia goniocarpa, also known as the scrub turpentine, is a plant species native to Australia, which has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and bark contain essential oils with turpentine-like properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Acronychia goniocarpa include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, skin conditions, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting respiratory health.
